metadata.title: States PCS: Maharashtra GK Questions for MPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which 15th-century Marathi Varkari saint-poet attained sainthood without a guru?
questions.explanation: Kanhopatra was a 15th-century Marathi Varkari saint-poet from Maharashtra. She is noted for attaining sainthood solely through devotion to Vithoba, without the guidance of any guru or lineage. Approximately 30 of her abhangas are extant. Her samadhi is located in Pandharpur, a major Varkari pilgrimage site.

questions.question: Which Sufi saint is associated with the 500-year-old Dargah located in Kalamnuri, Maharashtra?

options.label: D
options.text: Hazrat Sayyad Nooruddin Noori Shahid Chisti

answer.label: D
answer.text: Hazrat Sayyad Nooruddin Noori Shahid Chisti

questions.explanation: Kalamnuri in Maharashtra houses the 500-year-old Dargah of Hazrat Sayyad Nooruddin Noori Shahid Chisti, a revered Sufi saint. The site is in Hingoli district, at 19.67°N, 77.33°E, and stands 480 meters above sea level.

questions.question: Government Polytechnic Pen is located in which district of Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: Government Polytechnic Pen was established in 1990 in Pen, Raigad district, Maharashtra. Initially at Alibag, it later shifted to Pen. It offers diploma courses in engineering and technology under the Directorate of Technical Education, Maharashtra.

questions.question: Who was the 9th Peshwa famous for the Maratha Resurrection?
questions.explanation: Madhavrao I, 9th Peshwa (1761-1772), revived Maratha power after Panipat, defeated internal rivals and external enemies, restored Mughal Emperor Shah Alam II, refortified administration, and expanded the Maratha empire in North India, earning the period its name as the Maratha Resurrection. He died young from tuberculosis in 1772.

questions.question: Suyash Vishwas Gargate is associated with which sport?
questions.explanation: Suyash Vishwas Gargate, born 1997 in Pune, Maharashtra, is an all-rounder in India’s national kho kho team and played in the 2025 Kho Kho World Cup win. He has represented Maharashtra, Gujarat Giants, and Airports Authority of India, and has served as Sports Executive Officer (Training) for Maharashtra’s government.

questions.question: Which Mumbai temple is dedicated to the Panchkalshi community’s Kuldevi?
questions.explanation: Vajreshwari Temple in Mumbai is dedicated to the Kuldevi of the Panchkalshi community, also known as Somvanshi Kshatriya Pathare. The community is native to Mumbai and its distinct cultural traditions include the Pithori Amavasya festival.

questions.question: Who was the Gond Prince ruler of Tipagad Fort?
questions.explanation: Tipagad Fort in Gadchiroli, Maharashtra, was ruled by Gond Prince Puram Raja, known for commanding a strong force and defeating the King of Chhattisgarh. The fort is significant in Gond history and was abandoned after the mysterious death of Puram Raja and his queen.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: Kanhoji Jedhe was the founder of the Jedhe family. The forts of Raireshwar and Rohideshwar are associated with the Jedhes. Kanhoji Jedhe played a significant role in the rise of the Maratha Empire under Shivaji Maharaj. Raireshwar Fort was the place where Shivaji Maharaj took the oath of Hindavi Swarajya. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 2, 3 and 4 are correct. The Jedhe family is associated with the forts of Raireshwar and Rohideshwar, and Kanhoji Jedhe was instrumental in supporting Shivaji Maharaj during the establishment of the Maratha Empire. Raireshwar is historically notable for Shivaji’s oath of Hindavi Swarajya. However, Kanhoji Jedhe was not the founder of the Jedhe family; he was a prominent descendant.

questions.question: Who captured the bell from a Portuguese cathedral in 1707, which is now housed in the Meneshwar temple at Menavli in Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: The 650-kg bell at Meneshwar temple, Menavli, was captured by Chimaji Appa after his victory over the Portuguese at the Battle of Bassein in 1739. The bell features Christian motifs, symbolizing Maratha triumphs over Portuguese rule in Maharashtra.

questions.question: Who was awarded the title “Sena Dhurandhar” following the Battle of Pachgaon in Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: Mudhoji Bhonsle received the title “Sena Dhurandhar” after defeating his brother Sabaji Bhonsle at the Battle of Pachgaon (1775) near Nagpur, resolving the succession dispute in the Nagpur Kingdom of Maharashtra and securing regency for Raghoji II Bhonsle.
